waypoints.note: Pre-drop: aboard 38/46 Group RAF transport serials and Horsa/Hamilcar gliders forming over southern England. Position approximates the route start over the south coast — exact assembly geometry not material at division-level.
Coup-de-main party (Major Howard, D Coy 2nd Ox & Bucks LI) takes Pegasus Bridge by glider assault. Division centroid pinned to Pegasus at this instant; main brigade still en route over the Channel.

disputedBy.claim: Like the US airborne drops, 6th Airborne stick distribution on DZ-N was uneven; some sticks landed several kilometres off-DZ near Cabourg and Varaville. The DZ-N centroid is therefore an idealisation, not where most sticks actually came down.

waypoints.note: Main drop on DZ-N (Ranville–Bois-de-Bavent area): 5th Para Brigade (12, 13, 7 Para Bn). Position is the planned DZ-N centroid.

Ranville cleared by 13 Para Bn (first French village liberated by British forces); 9 Para (Otway) assaults Merville Battery (~04:30) with depleted force. Division centroid approximated on the Orne bridgehead.
Lord Lovat's 1st Special Service Brigade (from Sword) links up at Pegasus Bridge with Howard's coup-de-main party. Bridgehead consolidating; centroid drifts back to Pegasus at link-up moment.
End of MVP window: division holding the Orne bridgehead (Ranville–Bréville–Bois-de-Bavent line). Position is divisional centroid, approximate.
